metadata.claim_text: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
metadata.retrieval_notes: 7 sources retrieved from: factcheck, guardian, wikipedia, newsapi, livewiki.

nodes.text: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
nodes.short_explanation: Multiple authoritative sources — including NASA and scientific journals — confirm no astronaut has seen the Wall unaided from orbit. The wall is simply too narrow (15–30 ft wide) to resolve at orbital distances.

top_sources.title: Great Wall of China — Visibility from Space
top_sources.snippet: Visibility of the Great Wall from space has been a topic of discussion since the 1930s. In reality, it is not visible to the naked eye from low Earth orbit.

nodes.text: NASA confirms no astronaut has ever seen the Great Wall from space without optical aids.
nodes.short_explanation: NASA's official statement directly contradicts the claim — at orbital altitude the wall's width makes it physically impossible to resolve with the naked eye.

top_sources.title: Can You See the Great Wall of China from Space?
top_sources.snippet: Chinese astronaut Yang Liwei confirmed he could not see the Great Wall from space during his 2003 mission. The Wall is too narrow — about 15 to 30 feet wide — to be visible at orbital distances.

nodes.text: Snopes rates 'Great Wall visible from space' as FALSE. Multiple astronauts and NASA have denied the claim.
nodes.short_explanation: Professional fact-checking outlet rates this a debunked myth. The myth originated from a 1932 encyclopedia entry and has been perpetuated for decades despite no supporting evidence.

top_sources.title: Can the Great Wall of China Be Seen from Space?
top_sources.snippet: Rating: FALSE. The claim that the Great Wall of China is the only man-made structure visible from space is a popular myth. No astronaut has ever confirmed this with the naked eye.

nodes.text: Scientific American: angular resolution of the human eye makes the Wall invisible at ISS altitude of 408 km.
nodes.short_explanation: Physics-based analysis: at 400 km altitude, the human eye can resolve objects ~10 km wide. The Great Wall is only 5–9 m wide — roughly 1,000× too narrow to see.

top_sources.title: Great Wall of China: Why You Cannot See It From Space
top_sources.snippet: At the altitude of the International Space Station (408 km), the angular resolution needed to resolve the Great Wall is far beyond the capability of the human eye without optical aids.

nodes.text: China Daily reported astronaut claims of spotting landmark structures during low-altitude passes with favorable visibility.
nodes.short_explanation: A minority contrarian claim from Chinese state media — considered low credibility. No scientific peer review, nationalistic bias. Inconsistent with all other evidence.

top_sources.title: China Astronaut Reports Seeing Landmarks From Orbit
top_sources.snippet: A Chinese taikonaut reportedly observed large man-made structures during a low-altitude pass. Scientists caution the reports lack specifics about which structures were seen.

nodes.text: Human eye angular resolution is approximately 1 arcminute — limiting visible detail at distances beyond 100 km.
nodes.short_explanation: Contextual physics: the 1-arcminute resolution limit of the human eye is the key enabling condition. At 408 km altitude, minimum resolvable feature width is ~116 m — far greater than the Wall's 5–9 m width.

top_sources.title: Angular Resolution — Human Eye
top_sources.snippet: The human eye has a resolution of approximately 1 arcminute. At a distance of 400 km, this corresponds to a minimum resolvable feature of roughly 116 meters.

nodes.text: Commercial space tourism growth is bringing more civilian astronauts into orbit, generating fresh first-person visibility accounts.
nodes.short_explanation: Broader contextual signal: increased orbital tourism generates new visibility testimonies. None have reported seeing the Great Wall unaided, further corroborating the scientific consensus.

top_sources.title: What Space Tourists Are Saying About Earth's Details From Orbit
top_sources.snippet: As more civilians reach orbit with SpaceX and Blue Origin, accounts of Earth's visible features are multiplying. None have reported seeing the Great Wall unaided.

edges.label: refutes
edges.explanation: NASA directly contradicts the claim with official astronaut reports.

edges.label: fact-checked false
edges.explanation: Professional fact-check review rates the claim as definitively false.

edges.label: refutes
edges.explanation: Physics-based analysis proves visibility is impossible at orbital altitude.

edges.label: weak support
edges.explanation: Low-credibility source claims visibility — inconsistent with scientific consensus.

edges.label: context
edges.explanation: Angular resolution physics establishes the scientific basis for evaluating the claim.

edges.label: context
edges.explanation: Space tourism generates new testimonies relevant to the broader claim domain.
